creator: Alexander Terp
description:
Creating a circular reference (e.g. map containing itself) and then printing or comparing it causes a Go runtime stack overflow - a hard crash, not a recoverable error.

**Repro:**
```
m = {}
m["self"] = m
print(m)   // fatal error: stack overflow

a = [1]
a[0] = a
print(a)   // fatal error: stack overflow
```

No cycle detection exists in print or equality. Unlikely in normal usage but a hard crash is always bad.

**Test files:** `bugs/operators/64_self_referential.rad`, `65c_circular_list.rad`, `bugs/collections/87_list_contains_self.rad`, `91_list_self_ref_crash.rad`
title: Circular data structures cause stack overflow crash
